---
title: "Elena Tuzhilina"
---


![](photo.jpg){width=200px style="border-radius: 10%; float: right; margin-left: 1rem; margin-bottom: 1rem;"}

**Assistant Professor · Department of Statistical Sciences, University of Toronto**

📧 *elena.tuzhilina@utoronto.ca*

📍 *Office 9086, Ontario Power Building, Toronto*


I am an Assistant Professor in the [Department of Statistical Sciences](https://www.statistics.utoronto.ca/) 
at the University of Toronto since July 2022. My research focuses on developing statistical and computational methods for analyzing **complex, high-dimensional data**, with particular interests in **dimension reduction and data integration**. 
I am especially interested in developing methodology that is both theoretically grounded and practically useful, and I enjoy collaborating with scientists on applications in biology, medicine, and neuroscience.

I received my PhD in [Statistics from Stanford University](https://statistics.stanford.edu/) in 2022, 
supervised by [Professor Trevor Hastie](https://hastie.su.domains/). 
Before that, I studied pure mathematics at the [Faculty of Mechanics and Mathematics, Moscow State University](https://openday.msu.ru/https/opendaymsuru/math-en), and further developed my interest 
in machine learning and applied statistics through the [Yandex School of Data Analysis](https://dataschool.yandex.com/).
